author: Toasterbath
phrase_text: In Scott Pilgrim, we are introduced to the titular Scott Pilgrim and his rock band Sex Bob-omb, which he is the bassist for. Scott and his gay roommate Wallace Wells live in a tiny little rented space, where they sleep together in the same bed on the floor for the rest of the comic series.
error: where they sleep together in the same bed on the floor for the rest of the comic series.
correction: By the fourth installment, Scott and Wallace get kicked out of their tiny, Hobbit Hole-like space and for the rest of the series afterwards, Scott lives in Ramona's apartment.
notes: Sometimes I screw up about this stuff. Please email me corrections at yourseodorantsmellslikeskunk@gmail.com if I messed up
title: Scott Pilgrim

author: audiobuddy
phrase_text: Wizard of Chicago Harry Dresden has an erstwhile rival in mob boss John Marcone, but the two are on the same side surprisingly often. Marcone, increasingly supernatural-aware, has at times provided Dresden with free mercenary backup, helicopter transport, and mission intel, but he still insists that Dresden can't call him John.
error: provided Dresden with free mercenary backup
correction: Marcone didn't supply that backup for free. He gave it on the condition that Dresden sign him into the supernatural Accords (and that Dresden say "pretty please").
title: The Dresden Files

author: enottoA
phrase_text: Despite being a founding member of the team, X-Man Beast, aka Hank McCoy has done some morally questionable things. Among these deeds are leaving a vulnerable mutant with known villain Mister Sinister purposely, causing the 1906 San Francisco Earthquake, genetic warfare, destruction of multiple earths, cloning all 5 original X-Men and aging them up to teenagers, and working with an evil version of himself, literally named Dark Beast.
error: cloning all 5 original X-Men and aging them up to teenagers
correction: He didn't clone the X-Men, but rather used time travel to bring the past versions of themselves to the present day, as a way to teach them all a lesson.
title: The X-Men

author: InternallyDead
phrase_text: In Hades, Nyx, the goddess of the night, plays a significant role in Zagreus’ journey. As Zagreus’s biological mother, she serves as his caretaker and mentor, offering guidance and wisdom. Nyx is also responsible for helping Zagreus establish contact with the Olympians, using her influence to ensure their messages reach him despite Hades’ interference.
error: As Zagreus’s biological mother
correction: Nyx is not Zagreus’ biological mother - Persephone is. Nyx is his adoptive mother, having raised him in the Underworld after Persephone left
title: Hades the Game

author: bluArrowhead
phrase_text: In the core series of Pokémon games up to Generation 6, you obtain "Hidden Moves", also known as HMs, that can be taught to Pokémon to perform moves outside of battle. These HMs include HM01 Cut (which cuts down large trees), HM03 Surf (which allows you to cross bodies of water), and HM04 Strength (which can move large boulders).
error: "Hidden Moves", also known as HMs
correction: Um, Actually, HM stands for "Hidden Machine." "Hidden Moves" are the names of the moves taught by the HM.
title: Pokemon

author: bluArrowhead
phrase_text: In the world of One Piece, one of the most sought after treasures are Devil Fruits, which give supernatural abilities to whoever consumes them. These powers can range from giving your body the properties of rubber, generating fire, and even turning into animals. However, Devil fruits do have some rules to them: A person may only have 1 devil fruit ability and they lose the ability to swim in saltwater.
error: A person may only have 1 devil fruit ability
correction: Um, Actually, it is technically possible for someone to have 2 devil fruits. The pirate Blackbeard currently posses the powers of the Yami Yami no Mi and the Gura Gura no Mi, though it is currently unknown how he was able to do this.
title: One Piece

phrase_text: In Skyrim the game starts with you and a group of prisoners about to be executed however nobody gets executed do to the dragon Alduin attacking the village.
error: nobody gets executed
correction: One stormcloak gets executed before Alduin arrives.
title: Skyrim

phrase_text: Introduced in the Deep Space Nine episode, “The Search: Part 1”, the USS Defiant is officially classed as an escort ship, was designed as a prototype warship to fight the Borg, and due to this, only one Defiant-class ship was ever created.
error: and due to this, only one Defiant-class ship was ever created.
correction: Multiple Defiant-class ships have been created, including as a replacement for when the original got destroyed at the Second Battle of Chin’toka.
title: Star Trek

phrase_text: Introduced in the Deep Space Nine episode, “The Search: Part 1”, the USS Defiant is officially classed as an escort ship, was designed as a prototype warship to fight the Borg, and due to this, only one Defiant-class ship was ever created.
error: and due to this, only one Defiant-class ship was ever created.
correction: Multiple Defiant-class ships have been created, including as a replacement for when the original got destroyed at the Second Battle of Chin’toka.
title: Star Trek

author: CB Johnny
phrase_text: Prior to becoming a professional recording artist, "Weird Al" Yankovic enjoyed submitting amateur home recorded songs for the comedy radio program The Dr. Demento Show. There, his drummer Jon Schwartz met Al outside of the station's bathroom and offered to bang on his accordion case for a live in studio performance of My Bologna.
error: My Bologna.
correction: Another One Rides the Bus.
notes: The original single version of My Bologna was recorded in a college campus bathroom.
title: "Weird Al" Yankovic

phrase_text: League of Legends is a game consisting of many game modes, most notably taking place on Summoners Rift. This game mode is a 5v5 battle consisting of 3 lanes and jungle areas inbetween. Each lane contains 3 towers which you seek to destroy to eventually break the enemy nexus.
error: Each lane contains 3 towers which you seek to destroy
correction: Each lane actually contains 6 towers between the two teams.
title: League of Legends

phrase_text: League of Legends is a game consisting of many game modes, most notably taking place on Summoners Rift. This game mode is a 5v5 battle consisting of 3 lanes and jungle areas inbetween. Each lane contains 3 towers which you seek to destroy to eventually break the enemy nexus.
error: Each lane contains 3 towers which you seek to destroy
correction: Each lane actually contains 6 towers between the two teams.
title: League of Legends

author: TDean15
phrase_text: Nimona was originally a web comic made by ND Stevenson that was published in 2015 to great acclaim. After a troubled production, a film adaptation of the same name was released in 2023. The 2 versions have a significantly different story but both focus on self-proclaimed supervillain Ballister, and Nimona, a chaotic shapeshifter who wishes to become his sidekick.
error: 'self-proclaimed supervillain'
correction: Um, actually, Ballister is not a supervillain in the film version. He is instead an outlaw after he is accused of murdering the Queen.
title: Nimona

author: TDean15
phrase_text: In the Boiling Isles, where most of The Owl House takes place, witches are assigned to covens by being branded with a sigil at an early age. Examples of these are the Bard Coven and the Construction Coven. Any witch not in a coven is known a wild witch, outlaws who are capable of all forms of magic such as Eda who does not join a coven throughout the series.
error: 'who does not join a coven throughout the series'
correction: Um, actually, Eda does join a coven. She is covenless at the start of the series but is branded with a sigil in the final episode of Series 2 as part of a plan to stop a draining spell cast by Emperor Belos.
title: The Owl House

author: TDean15
phrase_text: Contrary to popular opinion, Princess Peach is not always a damsel in distress. Peach has been a playable character in several games including in Super Mario Land, Super Mario 3D World and Super Mario Bros Wonder. She has also received 2 games of her own, Super Princess Peach for the DS and Princess Peach: Showtime! for the Nintendo Switch.
error: *in Super Mario Land*
correction: Um, actually, Peach is not a playable character in Super Mario Land.
notes: Peach was not captured, Princess Daisy was in her first appearance, but Mario was the only playable character in the game.
title: Super Mario

author: ChaFerRod
phrase_text: While many days are eventful in this series, one of the most is Thursday, July 15, 1999. On this day, Jojo and co. face off against 4 stands, including Cheap Trick, Highway Star, Enigma, and Superfly.
error: Jojo and co. face off against 4 stands, including Cheap Trick, Highway Star, Enigma, and Superfly
correction: While 4 enemy stands are the center of Thursday, Highway Star is not fought on Thursday, he in fact helps Josuke to defeat Enigma. The 4th stand of significance is Killer Queen, which unlocks the Bites the Dust ability and activates it for the first time.
title: JoJo’s Bizarre adventure

author: Ico
phrase_text: In the game Super Mario Sunshine, Mario, along with peach, toadstool, and other toads, crash into Isle Delfino, an island filled with many touristical places, such as Delfino Plaza (the capital), Sirena beach and Pina Park
error: Crash into Isle delfino
correction: They don't crash into Isle Delfino, they safely land onto Delfino airstrip
title: Super Mario Sunshine.

phrase_text: Foster child Billy Batson meets a wizard who grants him the ability to transform into an adult superhero, with powers such as flight and super strength. However, he's still a child at heart, which explains why he likes to shout "Shazam" when he transforms, and also why one of the first things he did as a hero was try to buy beer.
error: However, he's still a child at heart, which explains why he likes to shout "Shazam" when he transforms, and also why one of the first things he did as a hero was try to buy beer.
correction: That's not why he shouts "Shazam!" Shazam is the name of the wizard who gave him the powers, and Billy must invoke his name to transform.
notes: Shazam is actually an acronym, representing the six gods which empower the given champion, being the wisdom of Solomon, the strength of Hercules, the stamina of Atlas, the lightning of Zeus, the courage of Achilles, and the speed of Mercury.
title: Shazam! (2019 Film)

author: Redmin
phrase_text: In Fallout: New Vegas, our player, the courier, is greeted by Benny with a monologue and a bullet to the head. Benny is the head of the Chairman whose family runs the Tops casino, one of the three casinos in the Vegas Strip that work under Mr. House. The other two casinos consist of the Ultra-Luxe, ran by the White Glove Society, and the Lucky 38, which serves as the base of operations for Mr. House and is closed to the public, except for the courier.
error: one of the three casinos in the Vegas Strip
correction: Um, actually the Vegas Strip has four casinos; the fourth being the Gomorrah, ran by the Omertas.
title: Fallout: New Vegas

author: SuperCJM
phrase_text: Among the three hidden opponents in Mortal Kombat II is Noob Saibot. Though his name is that of Mortal Kombat's creator, Tobias Boon, spelled backwards, it would later be revealed in Mortal Kombat: Deception that he is actually the undead Wraith form of Bi-Han, the original Sub-Zero.
error: Though his name is that of Mortal Kombat's creator, Tobias Boon, spelled backwards
correction: Mortal Kombat has two creators, Ed Boon and John Tobias, whose surnames spelled backwards form Noob Saibot's name
title: Mortal Kombat

author: SuperCJM
phrase_text: Among the three hidden opponents in Mortal Kombat II is Noob Saibot. Though his name is that of Mortal Kombat's creator, Tobias Boon, spelled backwards, it would later be revealed in Mortal Kombat: Deception that he is actually the undead Wraith form of Bi-Han, the original Sub-Zero.
error: Though his name is that of Mortal Kombat's creator, Tobias Boon, spelled backwards
correction: Mortal Kombat has two creators, Ed Boon and John Tobias, whose surnames spelled backwards form Noob Saibot's name
title: Mortal Kombat

author: SuperCJM
phrase_text: Walt Disney holds the record for both the most Oscar nominations and the most Oscar wins of any individual person, at 59 and 22, respectively. Though he was given Honorary Oscars for Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs and Fantasia, all of his competitive nominations were in the Documentary and Short Film categories.
error: all of his competitive nominations were in the Documentary and Short Film categories.
correction: He received one competitive Oscar nomination for a feature-length narrative film, that being a Best Picture nomination for Mary Poppins.
title: The Oscars

author: goldpuffle
phrase_text: Wood can be used to make many things, and different types of wood give rise to different textures of these things. There are some exceptions, though, such as bookshelves, paintings, chests, and crafting tables, which just default to one of the existing wood textures.
error: bookshelves, paintings, chests, and crafting tables, which just default to one of the existing wood textures.
correction: Paintings don't match the texture of any wood in the game and never have.
title: Minecraft

author: Yogsy
phrase_text: In House MD, the titular character Gregory House cycles through many residents. One resident was Amber Volakis, who despite no longer being an employee in the Princeton Plainsboro diagnostics department, returns to the show as the girlfriend of James Wilson before she passes away as a result of taking Zanamivir, a flu medication.
error: she passes away as a result of taking Zanamivir, a flu medication.
correction: Um, Actually, Amber takes Amantadine.
notes: Kind of a spoiler?
title: House MD

author: Yogsy
phrase_text: There are 177 episodes of House MD, following Gregory House, head of the diagnostics department of Princeton Plainsboro Teaching Hospital, and his team. A running joke in the show is that House has never had a case of Lupus, resulting in the popular meme of people saying "it's never lupus" when talking about the show. However, House did have a case of Lupus one time in season 4, episode 14, "Living the Dream", when a celebrity that House likes comes down with the disease.
error: in season 4, episode 14, "Living the Dream", when a celebrity that House likes comes down with the disease.
correction: Um, Actually, House's only case of Lupus is in season 4, episode 8, "You Don't Want To Know", where a magician at a show that Kutner and Cole are at contracts the disease.
title: House MD

author: QzMajor
phrase_text: In the 2012 version of the Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles, there is many villains who are human or aliens from other dimensions but there is also plenty of mutant villains as well. Bebop and Rocksteady, who were thieves turned into a rhinoceros and warthog respectively. Karai who was Shredder's daughter that was turned into a snake. and finally Dogpound (later known as Rahzar) and Fishface, who was Tim Bradford and Xever Montes prior to being mutated.
error: Karai who was Shredder's daughter
correction: Um actually Karai was not Shredder's daughter, Karai was the daughter of Hamato Yoshi who was stolen by Shredder as sort of revenge against Hamato Yoshi
title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles

author: QzMajor
phrase_text: In the 2012 version of the Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles, there is many villains who are human or aliens from other dimensions but there is also plenty of mutant villains as well. Bebop and Rocksteady, who were thieves turned into a rhinoceros and warthog respectively. Karai who was Shredder's daughter that was turned into a snake. and finally Dogpound (later known as Rahzar) and Fishface, who was Tim Bradford and Xever Montes prior to being mutated.
error: Karai who was Shredder's daughter
correction: Um actually Karai was not Shredder's daughter, Karai was the daughter of Hamato Yoshi who was stolen by Shredder as sort of revenge against Hamato Yoshi
title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles

author: Joekerr019
phrase_text: In the Kevin Smith movie "Clerks". We see the trials and tribulations of one Dante Hicks. From being forced to go to work on his free day,  to the gum jamming the shutters,  to a hockey game going wrong.  To even his co worker dale leaving Dante working  all day and going to Maine. To even finding out that his girlfriend thinks he is a pig for sleeping with seven girls while she went down on 37 guys
error: Co worker dale leaving Dante working all day and going up Maine
correction: Actually it was a guy called Snyder who went to Vermont
title: Clerks

author: DaxAttacks
phrase_text:
Sernuks are graceful deer-like creatures that symbolize honor, tradition, and longevity in the world of Palia.
Despite being accidentally introduced to the Kilima Ecosystem by Kenli (who thought they'd be good pets), The Order has happily accepted them and now even breed their own "Proudhorned Sernuk", to pull their carriages
error: Despite being accidentally introduced to the Kilima Ecosystem by Kenli
correction: Chapaas were the animals Kenli introduced, Sernuk are native to Kilima
title: Palia

author: DaxAttacks
phrase_text:
Sernuks are graceful deer-like creatures that symbolize honor, tradition, and longevity in the world of Palia.
Despite being accidentally introduced to the Kilima Ecosystem by Kenli (who thought they'd be good pets), The Order has happily accepted them and now even breed their own "Proudhorned Sernuk", to pull their carriages
error: Despite being accidentally introduced to the Kilima Ecosystem by Kenli
correction: Chapaas were the animals Kenli introduced, Sernuk are native to Kilima
title: Palia

author: darjella
phrase_text: In the video game Palia, humans are a legendary race that mysteriously went extinct thousands of years ago. As the player, you are the first human to be seen since then when you are located at the Phoenix Shrine by the archaeologist, Jina.
error: Um actually, you are just one of many reemerging humans.
correction: Palia is an MMO and canonically, every player is a reemerged human.
title: Palia

author: Dennish
phrase_text: Monster Hunter: World, is a videogame developed and published by the company Bandai Namco, that has been widely debated as to whether or not it can be considered a JRPG or an Action RPG. Monster Hunter: World does possess features of a JRPG, but unlike other JRPG titles such as Dragon Quest, Yakuza: Like A Dragon and Persona. It lacks the strong narrative focus and turn based combat. Rather, focusing on real-time combat, gear-based progression and grinding.
error: Monster Hunter: World, is a videogame developed and published by the company Bandai Namco
correction: Monster Hunter: World was actually developed and published by CAPCOM.
title: Monster Hunter: World

author: Memealicious
phrase_text: In the original 1989 run of the series Quantum Leap, Scott Bakula plays Dr. Sam Beckett. He’s a scientific prodigy who studies string theory, and in an experiment into time travel, he gains the ability for his conscious to travel to any point in time. However, he cannot control where he goes and only transfers into the body of other people. In his adventures, he ends up in the body of a black man in the 1950s South, a person with down syndrome, and a transgender escort.
error: he gains the ability for his conscious to travel to any point in time
correction: Um, Actually: he can only time travel to any point during his lifetime (1953-1989)
notes: In the 2022 Reboot, the main character Dr. Ben Song can travel to any point in history.
title: Quantum Leap

phrase_text: In the original 1989 run of the series Quantum Leap, Scott Bakula plays Dr. Sam Beckett. author: Memealicious
phrase_text: The Pixar movie “Finding Nemo” has a large scientific inaccuracy. The False Ocellaris Clownfish, which Marlin is based on, is a protogynous hermaphrodite. This means that since Nemo’s mother died, Marlin should have biologically changed into a female.
error: protogynous hermaphrodite
correction: Um Actually, They are not protogynous hermaphrodites, they are protandrous hermaphrodites.
notes: Protandrous hermaphrodites start as males and become females to increase fecundity (egg production).
title: Finding Nemo

author: Memealicious
phrase_text: Mel Gibson plays a former cop called Max Rockatansky in the Mad Max Series. The series details the story of this cop through the fall of society in Australia. It depicts a world after a resources war, with warring factions fighting over the precious resource of gasoline, water, and bullets. Despite this, these factions fight in tricked out cars including Max’s 1973 Ford Falcon Interceptor, and Furiosa’s War Rig semi-truck in Mad Max: Fury Road.
error: gasoline
correction: Um Actually, they’re fighting over Guzzolene not Gasoline.
notes: Society really falls quickly
title: Mad Max

author: Memealicious
phrase_text: In the Fallout series, Vault-Tec is the company that produces the vaults to preserve humanity after a nuclear war. However, most of these vaults were actually experiments on the human psyche, such as vaults pumped with hallucinogenic drugs, a vault with a man and a box of puppets, a vault with 999 men and one woman, a vault with 999 women and one man, and a vault where they elect a human sacrifice. The only vault without an experiment was vault 101 seen in Fallout 3.
error: The only vault without an experiment was vault 101 seen in Fallout 3.
correction: Um actually, Vault 101 did have an experiment, the vault was never supposed to open.
notes: Vault 101 is the origin of the Lone Wanderer, the main character of Fallout 3. The vault did not contain a G.E.C.K (Garden of Eden Creation Kit) to recolonize the outside world.
title: Fallout

author: Memealicious
phrase_text: In The Star Trek: The Next Generation Episode “Darmok”, The crew of the Starship Enterprise meets a ship of Tamarians. Even with the universal translator equipped from Star Fleet, the Tamarians are unable to be understood. This is because their grammar is based on metaphor, where the Tamarians use stories to convey meaning. Captain Picard learns this after the Tamarians strand him and their captain on a planet together.
error: metaphor
correction: Um Actually they communicate in allegory, not metaphor.
notes: Metaphors use direct comparisons to convey meaning, whereas allegory uses story to convey a meaning.
title: Star Trek

author: Memealicious
phrase_text: In Douglas Adams’ Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy, Arthur Dent and Ford Prefect are taken onto a Vogon Ship. Vogons are known as the bureaucrats of the universe. To torture people, they use poetry; Vogon poetry is the worst poetry in the universe, and the main characters are subjected to it before being thrown out of an airlock, only to be saved by the Infinite Improbability Drive of the Heart of Gold ship.
error: he worst poetry in the universe
correction: Um Actually, Vogon Poetry is the 3rd worst poetry in the universe.
notes: The Azgoths of Kria are the 2nd worst with poems like Ode to a Small Lump of Green Putty I Found in My Armpit One Midsummer Morning, and the absolute worst belongs to Paula Nancy Millstone Jennings of Sussex, who was destroyed when Earth was blown up to make a hyperspace expressway
title: Hitchiker's Guide to the Galaxy

author: Memealicious
phrase_text: The Walking Dead series is famous for never using the word “zombie”. Words like walkers, geeks, growlers, rotters, and Homo Mortis have been used instead of zombie. This is because within the universe of The Walking Dead, George A. Romero’s Night of the Living Dead was never made, and zombie was a word used in voodoo culture.
error: George A. Romero’s Night of the Living Dead was never made
correction: Um Actually, Night of the Living Dead was made, but sequels were never made, so there was never a widespread zombie culture to use the word.
notes: The movie was made; however, it was a cult film rather than a cultural touchstone of zombie media. The word comes from nzambi, meaning spirit of a dead person in many west african languages.
title: The Walking Dead

author: Memealicious
phrase_text: In the Smiling Friends episode “Who violently murdered Simon S. Salty”, there is a character named Grease that was originally meant to be played by the Internet lolcow Christine Weston Chandler. However, due to their criminal actions, Christine was unable to play the character. It was then played by Harry Partridge doing an impression of Christine.
error: due to their criminal actions
correction: Um actually, Chris-chan couldn’t play the character because of the show’s cameo budget.
notes: If you wanna know what happened, look it up, I'm not explaining it.
title: Smiling Friends

author: mantechcc
phrase_text: Full Metal Alchemist (2003) and Full Metal Alchemist :Brotherhood (2009) both adapt the Manga but the 2003 version focuses on the emotional impact of human transmutation and loss by introducing unique characters such as Nina, Dante, and Lyra.
error: unique characters such as Nina
correction: umm actually, Nina is in both the 2009 and 2003 anime adaptations
notes: 2003's FMA adaptation has a unique story line as it deviated from the Manga about half way through the story and created a unique ending before the Manga was completed.
title: Full Metal Alchemist

author: mantechcc
phrase_text: Full Metal Alchemist (2003) and Full Metal Alchemist :Brotherhood (2009) both adapt the Manga but the 2003 version focuses on the emotional impact of human transmutation and loss by introducing unique characters such as Nina, Dante, and Lyra.
error: unique characters such as Nina
correction: umm actually, Nina is in both the 2009 and 2003 anime adaptations
notes: 2003's FMA adaptation has a unique story line as it deviated from the Manga about half way through the story and created a unique ending before the Manga was completed.
title: Full Metal Alchemist

author: mantechcc
phrase_text: Homonculi are alchemically created humanoids in the Full Metal Alchemist universes. Full Metal Alchemist (2003), Full Metal Alchemist: Beotherhood (2009), and the Manga all feature the same seven homunculi, named after the seven deadly sins.
error: same seven homonculi
correction: the Manga's and 2009 anime's Wrath was called Pride in the 2003 anime. Also, Sloth and Wrath are completely different homonculi in the 2003 anime.
notes:
Sloth (2003) is the failed mother transmuataion of Alphonse and Edward Elric

Wrath (2003) is the failed son transmuatation of Izumi Curtis
title: Full Metal Alchemist

phrase_text: In Exploding Kittens, players will try to avoid drawing the Exploding Kitten using their ability cards, such as See the Future, Attack, Favor, Skip, and most importantly, Defuse. Moreover, some combinations of cards can also help with that, like two-of-a-kind, three-of-a-kind, and five-of-a-kind.
error: Moreover, some combinations of cards can also help with that, like two-of-a-kind, three-of-a-kind, and five-of-a-kind.
correction: Um, actually, five-of-a-kind is NOT a combination, what you actually want is five of the different types.
notes: There are other cards that are not in the base game, such as alter the future, swap top to bottom, draw from the bottom, and streaking kitten.
title: Exploding Kittens

phrase_text: The first trilogy of the Ace Attorney series revolves around Phoenix Wright, the protagonist, taking cases of innocent clients, usually accused of murder, although there was one time which the crime wasn't initially such, it was originally a robbery.
error: taking cases of innocent clients
correction: Even though at the beginning of the case, it was believed to be innocent, in Ace Attorney: Justice for All, the second game, in the last case, the client Matt Engarde is actually guilty of the crime.
title: Ace Attorney

phrase_text: The first trilogy of the Ace Attorney series revolves around Phoenix Wright, the protagonist, taking cases of innocent clients, usually accused of murder, although there was one time which the crime wasn't initially such, it was originally a robbery.
error: taking cases of innocent clients
correction: Even though at the beginning of the case, it was believed to be innocent, in Ace Attorney: Justice for All, the second game, in the last case, the client Matt Engarde is actually guilty of the crime.
title: Ace Attorney

author: RubyBlue42
phrase_text: In the 2006 film Idiocracy, average man Joe is frozen as part of a military experiment, and awakens 500 years later to discover that the average intelligence has plummeted and humans are now all crude and moronic.  After taking an IQ test in prison, Joe is eventually appointed Secretary of Agriculture by President Dwayne Elizondo Mountain Dew Herbert Camacho, and must improve crop yields nation-wide, or face imprisonment.
error: Secretary of Agriculture
correction: Joe is appointed Secretary of the Interior, not Agriculture.
notes: The Secretary of the Interior would not normally be the one tasked with improving crop yields, but President Camacho isn't that smart.
title: Idiocracy

author: MisterBrick42
phrase_text: In Content Warning, an April Fools parody of Lethal Company, you play as a gang of content creators who must take a Diving Bell to the Old World, where you record various monsters for clout, such as a zombie snail, a statue that moves when you aren’t looking, a ghost with a knife, or a moving cage that can trap your teammates and force you to solve a Captcha to get them out.
error: A statue that moves when you aren’t looking, ***OR*** a moving cage that can trap your teammates and force you to solve a Captcha to get them out.
correction: The statue and the cage are actually THE SAME monster.
notes: It’s also not actually a Captcha, it’s a “Catchya.” Because it did in fact Catchya.
title: Content Warning

author: MisterBrick42
phrase_text: In Content Warning, an April Fools parody of Lethal Company, you play as a gang of content creators who must take a Diving Bell to the Old World, where you record various monsters for clout, such as a zombie snail, a statue that moves when you aren’t looking, a ghost with a knife, or a moving cage that can trap your teammates and force you to solve a Captcha to get them out.
error: A statue that moves when you aren’t looking, ***OR*** a moving cage that can trap your teammates and force you to solve a Captcha to get them out.
correction: The statue and the cage are actually THE SAME monster.
notes: It’s also not actually a Captcha, it’s a “Catchya.” Because it did in fact Catchya.
title: Content Warning

author: apetopac
phrase_text: Beyond the titular no-armed whitey, Free Country, USA is home to all sorts of weird animal things. Among the 12 most common characters, there's Strong Bad, the boxing gloved, wrestle masked antagonist-turned-slight-annoyance, Pom Pom, Homestar's "best friend and confidant" and town hunk, and Marzipan, Homestar's girlfriend who only dates him because he's the only resident that she can stand the company of.
error: best friend and confidant
correction: Um, actually, Homestar describes Pom Pom as his "best friend and concubine", and follows that statement with feeling the need to look up the definition of the second word.
title: Homestar Runner

author: What do you mean? There's no auth.
phrase_text: The acclaimed 2018 board game Root: A Woodland Game of Might and Right has received a large number of expansions to expand its roster of fully asymmetric animal factions. Players today can participate in a war with factions based on woodland animals such as adventuring raccoons, terrorist crows, industrialist cats, diasporic toads and peace-advocating bats.
error: cats
correction: Unlike the other listed animals, cats are distinctively *not* woodland animals.
notes: In fact, in the game's loose lore, the whole war started because cats are an invasive species coming from elsewhere (presumably urban) to disrupt the woodland's power structure in an attempt to harvest wood and industrialize.
title: Root

author: Bracht
phrase_text: In the 11th episode of the 4th Season of Glee, the New Directions group performs Sir Mix-a-Lot raunchy hit "Baby Got Back" in a surprisingly mellow rendition that could easily pass for a romantic song if you don't pay attention to the lyrics. However, audiences soon noticed this curious arrangement had in fact been released 7 years earlier by indie comedy singer-songwriter Jonathan Coulton (who incidentally composed both of the songs sang by GLaDOS in the credits of both Portal games). The Glee version was uncannily similar, even including the line "Johnny C.'s in trouble", which is only present in the Jonathan Coulton release — the original song said "Mix-a-Lot's in trouble" instead. Glee never credited Coulton, even after the song was made available for purchase on iTunes and included in a Glee album.
error: the New Directions group performs
correction: Um, actually… the song was performed in the episode by the Adam's Apples group, not by the protagonist New Directions group.
title: Glee / Jonathan Coulton

author: ragingdawn
phrase_text: In monster hunter, you can use various weapons like greatsword, sword and shield, blade that can be turned into axe, and music instrument? You heard it correct, hunting horn is a blunt weapon like hammer but you can buff yourself and ally which includes increasing attack, defense, movement speed up, mind's eye (which your weapon cannot bounce), and negating poison.
error: movement speed up and mind's eye
correction: movement speed up and mind's eye only affecting the self-horn user which is called Self Improvement
title: Monster Hunter

author: caoenqi
phrase_text: Long before "The Traitors" graced our screens, "The Mole" was one of the first mainstream reality competitions to revolve around a hidden traitor mechanic. In the show, contestants complete missions to earn money towards a group pot, while secretly being sabotaged by the titular "Mole": a contestant chosen and assisted by production, who wins any money the group fails to earn. Eliminations each episode are determined by a quiz about the "Mole", with the lowest scorer being sent home.
error: who wins any money the group fails to earn.
correction: The Mole doesn't earn any money through gameplay; they're sabotaging (and trying to remain undetected) for the pure love of the game.
notes: Sure, the Netflix version of the show started in 2022, which would only put it a year ahead of "The Traitors", but the original first season actually aired way back in 2001!
title: The Mole

author: #SuperFinnee
phrase_text: On April 1st, 2016, Taylor Swift released an advertisement for Apple Music where she is seen running on a treadmill expertly singing to Drake's new song "One Dance." As she passionately sings, Taylor trips and falls off the treadmill, lying on the floor, then continues to sing. Drake also releases an advertisement, where he is singing and dancing along to Taylor Swift's song "Bad Blood" while in the gym.
error: Drake's new song "One Dance."
correction: Um, actually, she was singing along to Drake and Future's song "Jumpman"
title: Taylor Swift

author: #SuperFinnee
phrase_text: Taylor Swift's "the last great american dynasty" follows the story of Rebekah Harkness, a patron of the arts, and founder of the Rebekah Harkness Foundation. The song goes on to describe a feud between Rebekah and her neighbour, where "She stole his dog and dyed it tangerine." In actuality, Rebekah stole her neighbour's cat, which may be a reflection on how folklore and stories mutate as they're passed between generations
error: "She stole his dog and dyed it tangerine."
correction: Um, Actually, the correct lyric is "She stole his dog and dyed it key lime green"
title: Taylor Swift

author: #SuperFinnee
phrase_text: In Taylor Swift's song "Death By A Thousand Cuts" she compares a possible breakup to a slow, painful death. In the bridge, "My heart, my hips, my body, my love" are parts of Taylor this person irreversibly touched. Similarly, she lists "My time, my wine, my spirit, my fuss" trying to find a part of her that this person didn't take up.
error: "My time, my wine, my spirit, my fuss"
correction: Um, Actually, This unknown person took up her trust, not her fuss.
title: Taylor Swift

phrase_text: In The Truman Show, there is a flashback to Truman's education, where he is alone away from the crowd, and encounters Sylvia, who's stage name is Lauren. She is adamant that they must do what they want to do now, because they won't get a chance later. They escape the cameras briefly and run into the forest bordering Seahaven for a short moment of authenticity, kissing passionately. Christof returns control to himself by sending in a man who Sylvia has never seen before to remove her from Truman, and ultimately from the set of The Truman Show, leaving behind only the memory, and her red sweater.
error: run into the forest bordering Seahaven
correction: Um, Actually, Truman leads Sylvia towards the beach, and seems to be able to dodge the cameras very well, foreshadowing his future escape.
title: The Truman Show

author: #SuperFinnee
phrase_text: One of the main turning points of The Truman Show is when the audience and the Moon Room discover that Truman has escaped through a tunnel dug from inside a cupboard, leading to the backyard. Marlon, who was looking for Truman, climbs up the hole and looks directly down the barrel of a camera to tell Christof "He's Gone." This camera is the same one where we saw Truman earlier in the movie doing gardening in that exact spot, to then show Meryl advertising to Truman (but actually the audience) to get a new "Elk Rotary." This indicates Truman was aware of his captivity and planning his escape well before the movie began.
error: Meryl advertising to Truman (but actually the audience) to get a new "Elk Rotary."
correction: Um, Actually, Meryl was advertising for the "Chef's Pal" which is a dicer, grater and peeler, that never needs sharpening and is dishwasher safe!
title: The Truman Show

author: #SuperFinnee
phrase_text: In May 2022, TfL officially opened the city section of the Elizabeth line, but one station – Tottenham Court Road – had not been completed on time due to tunnelling issues, fire safety and protection standards changes and the COVID-19 pandemic.
error: Tottenham Court Road
correction: Um, Actually, Bond Street was the station plagued with delays, although TfL did a good job getting it back on track, only ending up 5 months delayed from the rest of the line.
title: The London Underground

author: #SuperFinnee
phrase_text: The Victoria Line is unique from other lines, since it is the only tube line with more than two stops to have an interchange at every station. It was fully opened in 1971 between Brixton and Walthamstow Central Station, stopping at busy stations including Victoria, Green Park and Kings Cross St Pancras
error: only tube line with more than two stops to have an interchange at every station.
correction: Um, Actually, Pimlico is the only station on the Victoria line to not have an interchange recognised by TfL.
title: The London Underground

phrase_text: WWE's King of the Ring, while not held every year during its existence, has been a long-standing tournament since 1993 when it was first won by Bret "Hitman" Hart. Since then, Kings of the Ring have included Ken Shamrock, Wade Barrett, Xavier Woods, Billy Gunn (who was known as "Mr. Ass" at the time), and Bret's youngest brother, Owen.
error: WWE's King of the Ring, while not held every year during its existence, has been a long-standing tournament since 1993
correction: Um, actually...the King of the Ring tournament has been around since 1985; it was mostly held at house shows and thus wasn't much promoted on then-WWF television. The first King of the Ring was Don Muraco.
title: Professional Wrestling

author: CowAboutThat
phrase_text: Theoretical physicist Dr. Gordon Freeman is the primary protagonist of the Half-Life series, but other spin-off games put you in control of other protagonists. Blue Shift introduces security guard Barney Calhoun, who returns as an NPC in Half-Life 2, and Opposing Force introduces Adrian Shepard, a US Marine Corporal. Both spin-offs take place in the same location and roughly the same time frame as the original game.
error: Blue Shift introduces security guard Barney Calhoun
correction: Um, Actually, in the original Half-Life, while Gordon is on the tram, he can see Barney knocking on a security door, so Barney was already introduced before he got his own game.
notes: https://half-life.fandom.com/wiki/Barney_Calhoun
title: Half-Life

author: CowAboutThat
phrase_text: In HBO’s Game of Thrones spin-off House of the Dragon, based on George R.R. Martin’s Fire and Blood, King Viserys Targaryen (played by Paddy Considine) breaks many years of precedent by naming his daughter Rhaenyra (played by Emma D’Arcy) as his successor. He remains firm in this choice, even when he later has a legitimate male heir, Aegon (played by Tom Glynn-Carney), who many believe has a stronger claim to the Iron Throne.
error: breaks many years of precedent by naming his daughter Rhaenyra (played by Emma D’Arcy) as his successor
correction: Um, Actually, at the point when Rhaenyra is named as his successor, the character is being played by Milly Alcock and is not replaced by D’Arcy until episode 6.
title: House of the Dragon

author: CowAboutThat
phrase_text: In the hit indie metroidvania Hollow Knight, players control a vessel designed to have “No mind to think. No will to break. No voice to cry suffering,” but there are hints that he is less hollow than intended. He can use the Void Heart charm, which explicitly says that it binds to the user’s will, proving that the Hollow Knight does actually have a will.
error: He can use the Void Heart charm, which explicitly says that it binds to the user’s will, proving that the Hollow Knight does have a will
correction: Um, Actually, while the player character was intended to be hollow, they are not the Hollow Knight. The Hollow Knight is their sibling who was chosen as a better fit. The player character is referred to as “the knight” or “Ghost.” Also, the vessels are considered genderless due to their hollowness, so using he/him pronouns to refer to the character is generally considered inaccurate.
title: Hollow Knight

author: CowAboutThat
phrase_text: Hearthstone’s Battlegrounds mode changes up the usual collectible trading card gameplay style by pitting you against 7 other players and having you build a team of minions without knowing exactly what your next opponent has on their board. When recruiting, building, and improving your team, the only information you have about your opponent’s team is their most common minion type (and how many they have of that type) and your memory of previous encounters with them, meaning you have to choose your minions’ actions on the fly during the battle phase. Fortunately, placing anywhere in the top 4 is considered a win by the game.
error: meaning you have to choose your minions’ actions on the fly during the battle phase
correction: Um, Actually, players have no control over their minions during the battle phase. Minions attack mostly randomly.
title: Hearthstone

author: CowAboutThat
phrase_text: Supermassive Games develops the horror series “The Dark Pictures Anthology.” Beginning with their hit game Until Dawn, The Dark Pictures games task players with making decisions for a whole cast of characters, all played by you or split among a group of friends. Choose well, and your characters can improve their relationships with each other while avoiding horrors. Choose poorly, and some of your characters may not make it to the final credits.
error: series “The Dark Pictures Anthology.” Beginning with their hit game Until Dawn, The Dark Pictures games
correction: Um, Actually, even though Until Dawn has nearly identical gameplay and was Supermassive’s breakout game, it’s not part of The Dark Pictures Anthology.
title: Supermassive Games

author: merkon
phrase_text: In the hit YA book series Animorphs, the main villain Visser 3 (later promoted to Visser 1) is a brain-controlling slug named Esplin 9266. Esplin famously controls the Andalite Alloran-Semitur-Corrass, but also has controlled the bodies of multiple Hork Bajir, a Gedd, and a Human.
error: Visser 3's correct name
correction: Esplin 9466, not 9266
title: Animorphs

author: CowAboutThat
phrase_text: Myst is an iconic point-and-click adventure game set partly on Myst Island, where Atrus wrote some of his linking books, which can send people to other worlds called “Ages.” To beat the game, players must travel to several of these Ages to get the information required to complete the game back on Myst Island.
error: To beat the game, players must travel to several of these Ages
correction: Um, Actually, if you already know the code in the fireplace, you can finish the game in a manner of minutes without ever leaving Myst Island.
title: Myst

author: merkon
phrase_text: In the classic YA book series Animorphs, five teenages and one Andalite teenage famously turn into animals to fight invading brain slugs. In addition to the Animorphs, some other people along the way acquire the morphing power as well- a new kid at their school named David, a group of disabled teenagers, a buffalo, an ant, and their vice principal. Unfortunately, everyone else that gains access to the morphing ability is ultimately killed.
error: who is given access to the morphing ability
correction: their vice principal is not giving the morphing power
notes: David is killed at the end of book 48 by Rachel, the auxiliary animorphs are killed by Visser 3/1 in book 54, and the buffalo and ant are killed in the book they're introduced- book 39.
title: Animorphs

author: CowAboutThat
phrase_text: John-117, better known as Master Chief, is the protagonist of most Halo games. He has secured many crucial victories against the alien coalition, the Covenant, but at what cost? John, like his fellow Spartan 2’s, was kidnapped as a young child, leaving his caretakers to wonder what happened to him, and indoctrinated into obedient military servitude.
error: leaving his caretakers to wonder what happened to him
correction: Um, Actually, the kidnapped Spartan 2 children were replaced with clones that would quickly fall ill and die so that caretakers wouldn’t know the kids were kidnapped.
title: Halo

author: ozyzero
phrase_text: The tabletop actual play series Dimension 20: A Starstruck Odyssey features a campaign in a sci-fi universe originally co-created by Elaine Lee and Michael Kaluta, following the adventures of a crew of a ragtag space ship trying to save the galaxy. Notably, this was the first Dimension 20 Campaign to use a system other than D&D 5th Edition.
error: Notably, this was the first Dimension 20 Campaign to use a system other than D&D 5th Edition.
correction: Misfits & Magic and Shriek Week had both used systems other than D&D 5e (Kids on Brooms and Mythic, respectively), and both were released prior to A Starstruck Odyssey, which uses the Star Wars 5e role-playing system.
title: Dimension 20

author: CowAboutThat
phrase_text: The foppish bard Volo can be found early in Baldur’s Gate 3, being forced to be a jester for a camp of goblins. Later, he may offer to attempt to surgically remove the mind flayer parasite through your eye. As this starts to go wrong, if you continue allowing him to work despite repeated warnings, Volo will accidentally carve your eye out, leading to vision penalties until you can find a fix.
error: leading to vision penalties until you can find a fix
correction: This one actually has two corrections. Um, Actually, despite seeming very bard-like, Volo is a wizard, and Um, Actually, Volo immediately gives you a magic replacement eye as an apology that permanently lets your character see invisibility, so it’s actually a huge improvement to have Volo cut out your eye.
title: Baldur’s Gate 3

author: CowAboutThat
phrase_text: Papers, Please puts players in the shoes of a border control agent who must verify the accuracy of various immigration documents for citizens or foreigners hoping to travel into Arstotzka. Follow all your corrupt government’s constantly changing instructions, and you’ll be promoted to a higher rank, but there are several other endings too. You can help a resistance group pass into the country to overthrow the government, your entire family can starve to death, you can escape to Obristan, or 14 different ways to be arrested or executed. Glory to Arstotzka!
error: Follow all your corrupt government’s constantly changing instructions, and you’ll be promoted into a higher rank
correction: Um, Actually, if you follow ALL the instructions, including to turn over any evidence you have of the resistance group, that will also get you arrested for suspicion of collaborating with them, and even if you follow most of their instructions, you just unlock endless mode and continue at your same rank.
title: Papers, Please

author: CowAboutThat
phrase_text: The X-COM series began in 1994 with UFO: Enemy Unknown. After that, the series came to be known as X-COM instead of UFO, and was rebooted after an 11 year hiatus as XCOM: Enemy Unknown. The games put you in control of XCOM, the paramilitary force tasked with fighting off an alien invasion.
error: tasked with fighting off an alien invasion
correction: Um, Actually, not all the games have you fighting off an invasion. In XCOM 2, the alien invasion is long past, and the aliens have won. You’re trying to overthrow them. And XCOM: Chimera Squad has you in charge of combined alien-human task force in a city.
title: X-COM

author: CowAboutThat
phrase_text: In the Hitman series, you play as Agent 47, an assassin employed by the International Contract Agency known for his ability to blend in across any environment by disguising himself - sometimes in the clothes of his own victims. In the most recent World of Assassination trilogy, Agent 47 and his handler Diana Burnwood track down information to topple Providence, the shadowy organization that trained 47. They dig up elements of his past, including the Institute for Human Betterment where he was raised and trained, his birth name Tobias Rieper, and Lucas Grey, an old ally also raised by Providence whom 47 knew as a child before having his memory tampered with.
error: his birth name Tobias Rieper
correction: Um, actually, Tobias Rieper (pronounced “reaper”) is just a very on-the-nose alias that Agent 47 often uses. Agent 47 is a clone, the 47th clone made, so he has no birth name.
title: Hitman

author: CowAboutThat
phrase_text: The Arkham Horror Files tabletop games were inspired by RPG Call of Cthulhu, so you know you’re going to stumble across some nasty monsters and locales of both the New England and otherworldly varieties during your playthroughs. They began with the Arkham Horror card game, but later entries to the series include a dice game, Elder Sign; and two board games: Betrayal at House on the Hill, which even has a companion app, and Eldritch Horror. All four games, unless played solo, are cooperative.
error: Betrayal at House on the Hill, which even has a companion app
correction: Um, actually, the first board game described above is Mansions of Madness. Betrayal at House on the Hill is a similarly themed horror board game that also has a companion app but is not in the Arkham Horror Files.
title: Arkham Horror Files

author: #SuperFinnee
phrase_text: When the Jubilee Line first opened, the line terminated at Charing Cross, and the tunnels are still around today, disused. In 1999, the Jubilee Line extension opened, linking Stanmore to Stratford via the Docklands. This extension included platform screen doors between Westminster and Stratford, to prevent delays and keep passengers safe.
error: This extension included platform screen doors between Westminster and Stratford
correction: Um, Actually, there are no platform screen doors on the above-ground section of the Jubilee line extension, including: Canning Town, West Ham, and Stratford
title: The London Underground

author: Loyd
phrase_text: In Minecraft, the Ender Dragon is the only boss that naturally spawns in the game. Defeating it for the first time opens the exit portal and causes a dragon egg to appear on top of the exit portal, and every time you respawn and defeat the Ender Dragon again, another dragon egg is generated.
error: Every time you respawn and defeat the Ender Dragon again, another dragon egg is generated.
correction: Only the first Ender Dragon defeat generates a dragon egg. Respawned Ender Dragons don’t create additional dragon eggs.
title: Minecraft

author: WhatTheDevin
phrase_text: The concept of romance in troll society is quite a bit broader than that of humans, consisting of four distinct relationship dynamics, known as quadrants, that trolls are are expected to seek partners for simultaneously, each represented by a different playing card suit: The flushed quadrant, represented by a heart  is analogous to human romance, the ichorous quadrant, represented by a spade, consists of a vicious rivalry (but with boning), the pale quadrant, consisting of a diamond, consists of a platonic "best friends" dynamic, and the ashen quadrant, represented by a club, consists of two "would-be" rivals and a mediator.
error: the ichorous quadrant
correction: this quadrant should be called the caliginous quadrant.
notes: Players might guess that the error relates to the fact that human characters take interest in quadrants later in the comic, and fully adopt them into their shared-species society in the epilogues. However, the concept of quadrants still originates with trolls, and has not been shown to exist in troll-less societies, so this fact does not contradict the statement.
title: Homestuck

author: Ymbirtt
phrase_text: Being a sci-fi series, reloading is a thing of the past in the first Mass Effect game. Rather than shooting physical projectiles, most guns are energy weapons which accumulate heat during firing, and cool down passively without any player interaction. For gameplay reasons, though, Mass Effects 2 and 3 switch back to using disposable ammo magazines, which the player needs to reload just like in any other game. This gets charmingly lampshaded by a character in Mass Effect 3
error: disposable ammo magazines
correction: They're not disposable ammo magazines, they're disposable heatsinks called "Thermal Clips". Your guns do still overheat, but they're now cooled down by ejecting and replacing a disposable heatsink. It's mechanically identical to just reloading a magazine, but this way is more sci-fi!
notes:
There are actually two things wrong with this statement!

For extra credit: in fact, guns in Mass Effect generally do shoot physical projectiles! As the codex explains, though, guns are "loaded" with large metal ammo blocks, and fire by ripping tiny shards of metal off those blocks.

If a player catches this extra credit correction, they should steal the point from anyone who catches the "disposable ammo magazines" correction.
title: Mass Effect

author: Ymbirtt
phrase_text: Twilight Imperium is an epic board game, which wears its Star Wars influences on its sleeve. Just like in Star Wars, players do battle with fleets of varied ships, from the lowly cruiser to the mighty destroyer, meet at the galactic council, designating holy planets and  sanctioning rivals, and trade goods and promises among each other, keeping their allies wealthy and their enemies poor.
error: the lowly cruiser to the mighty destroyer
correction: Cruisers are actually much larger ships than destroyers
notes: You don't need to know anything about Twilight Imperium for this: in real life, destroyers are relatively small warships. George Lucas doesn't use military terminology very precisely, and the "Imperial Star Destroyer" is likely called a destroyer because it sounds cool. He also doesn't seem to understand what a general is.
title: Twilight Imperium

author: CowAboutThat
phrase_text: Super Meat Boy is the hero of his titular precise platforming game, with a similar premise to some of the Mario games: his romantic interest has been kidnapped, but each time he thinks he’s gotten her, he finds out he needs to continue to progress through the world. Super Meat Boy gets a bit weirder, though, as his damsel in distress, Bandage Girl, was taken by the evil Dr. Fetus, and Super Meat Boy is bound to gush blood across dozens of saw blades as he avoids death against villains such as C.H.A.D., a massive blob of animated blood; Lil Slugger, a chainsaw mech designed for deforestation; Brownie, a sprinkled dessert come to life; and Dr. Fetus himself, a literal fetus in a tank with a suit and tie.
error: Brownie, a sprinkled dessert come to life
correction: Um, actually, Brownie is one of Dr. Fetus’s turds come to life, and he actually ends up being more of an antihero than a villain when he saves Super Meat Boy’s life.
title: Super Meat Boy

phrase_text: In 2005, WWE introduced the concept of "Money in the Bank", a privilege that the winner can use to challenge for any championship in the company that they choose, without even so much as warning, for a period of one year after winning it. Among those who have "cashed in" on this privilege are Edge, Alexa Bliss, Brock Lesnar, Bayley, Drew McIntyre, and Nikki Cross. Among all winners, only two have ever won the match twice: CM Punk and Seth Rollins.
error: Among all winners, only two have ever won the match twice: CM Punk and Seth Rollins.
correction: In 2018, Carmella won the first women's MITB ladder match, but only because a man, James Ellsworth, climbed the ladder, grabbed the briefcase, and dropped it into her hands. While this was an accepted victory, SmackDown General Manager Daniel Bryan stripped her of the privilege and ordered a re-do on the next episode of SmackDown. This time, Carmella won without Ellsworth's help.
title: Professional Wrestling

author: CowAboutThat
phrase_text: The Game of Thrones finale came stutteringly, with some fans complaining it did too much fan service, other fans complaining it didn’t pay off the fans’ investment enough, and plenty of others complaining about both. Nevertheless, it gave accolades to series’ favorites when Tyrion was the one to officially suggest the next regent of Westeros, the magnanimous and not particularly power-hungry Bran the Broken. While most of the seven kingdoms agreed to this accord and swore fealty, Yara Greyjoy and Sansa Stark insisted on independence for the Iron Islands and Winterfell, respectively, based on their fraught relationship with the Iron Throne. The new king, Bran, accepted independence without complaint.
error: While most of the seven kingdoms agreed to this accord and swore fealty, Yara Greyjoy and Sansa Stark insisted on independence for the Iron Islands
correction: Um, actually, even though it made no sense for the character of Yara or the history of the Iron Islands, Yara swore allegiance and didn’t change her mind even after Sansa declared independence from her own brother.
title: Game of Thrones

author: CowAboutThat
phrase_text: When Jon Snow voluntarily took the black, becoming a member of the Night’s Watch, he never expected to be a royal war leader and leave the Wall, an action he could only justify by dying for some time. He fights, in part, on behalf of his half-siblings in the Battle of the Bastards: bastard Jon against bastard Ramsay, illegitimate son of Lord Roose Bolton. Jon defeats Ramsay, but he lets his half-sister be the one to actually give the cretinous Ramsay his death sentence.
error: bastard Jon against bastard Ramsay, illegitimate son of Lord Roose Bolton
correction: Um, actually, by the time the battle of the bastards occurs, Ramsay Bolton has been legitimized by Roose, and it is later revealed that Jon is actually not a bastard either, so neither of the commanders in the Battle of the Bastards are bastards by Westerosi standards.
title: Game of Thrones

author: CowAboutThat
phrase_text: Someone’s sensitivity to the Force has been a fraught issue for the Star Wars fandom. Early fans were shocked to discover that Luke Skywalker’s Force sensitivity was related to his evil father Darth Vader, but many fans expressed discontent when The Phantom Menace revealed Darth Vader (at the time Anakin) had Force sensitivity due to midichlorians. Trilogy episodes 7-9 made it clear that Force sensitivity was not based on bloodlines, when Kylo Ren made Rey admit that she knew her parents were “nobody.”
error: Trilogy episodes 7-9 made it clear that Force sensitivity was not based on bloodlines
correction: Um, actually, episode 9 reverses the setup and suddenly makes Rey a granddaughter of Palpatine, completely invalidating the idea that anyone has access to the Force.
title: Star Wars

author: CowAboutThat
phrase_text: Romance reality show Temptation Island focuses on couples not sure how to continue their relationships. The couples are separated and surrounded by attractive singles, but every once in a while, Mark Wahlberg (Marky Mark himself), shows the separated couples incriminating clips of their time with the other singles. As might be expected, many couples realize they should leave their relationships, while some decide to leave with a new partner.
error: Mark Wahlberg (Marky Mark himself)
correction: Um, actually, the host of Temptation Island is Mark L. Walberg, a fully different person from actor Mark Wahlberg.
title: Temptation Island

author: mjr
phrase_text: Bethesda’s Fallout 3 takes place in Washington DC, far from Fallout's west coast origins, but contains familiar enemies.  These include mutated creatures such as super mutants (from Vault 87), ghouls (radiation survivors hideously disfigured by radiation) and deathclaws (mutated chameleons formed in the radiation from the bombs in 2077).
error: deathclaws (mutated chameleons formed in the radiation from the bombs in 2077).
correction: Deathclaws are actually a pre-war genetic engineering experiment by the US Army.
notes: https://fallout.fandom.com/wiki/Deathclaw
title: Fallout

author: mjr
phrase_text: In Outer Wilds, the player character becomes stuck in a 22 minute time loop. Through exploring, the player learns about an ancient race of being called the Nomai, who were obsessed with finding The Eye Of The Universe.  The time loop is caused when an ancient Sun Station finally functions, triggering a supernova and destroying the solar system.  This energy from the supernova sends the travelers consciousness back in time 22 minutes to explore again.
error: The time loop is caused when an ancient Sun Station finally functions
correction: The sun station never functions.  The sun station was intended to cause a supernova to power the Ash Twin Project, but was unable to do so.  The Ash Twin project functions because the sun (and all other stars) are naturally going supernova at the end of the universe.
notes: https://outerwilds.fandom.com/wiki/Sun_Station
title: Outer Wilds

phrase_text: Fallout 4 marks the first appearances of Synths, which are faux-humans created by The Institute.  While earlier generation synths like Nick Valentine appear clearly robotic, Third generation synths are highly advanced and often pass as humans.  Some synths are sleeper agents and are not even aware themselves that they are synths.
error: Fallout 4 marks the first appearances of Synths
correction: A synth appears in Fallout 3, in the quest The Replicated Man
notes: https://fallout.fandom.com/wiki/The_Replicated_Man
title: Fallout

author: Witherblaze
phrase_text: Sure we all know Dracula, but what is his actual name?  Well, in Lament of Innocence, we find out that Dracula was originally a human name Matthias Cornquist, a tactitican who worked with Leon Belmont during the 11th century.  Matthias's decides to beecome a vampire after he accuses God of unjustly taking away his wife, Elisabetha, from him.  With the Crimson stone in hand and taking the soul of a vampire named Walter, he would later earn the names Vlad Tepes aka Dracula.  However, those aren't the only names associated with the count.  In Castlevania 64, he disguises himself as a man named Gilles de Rais.  After Dracula dies in 1999, he would eventually be reincarnated as Soma Cruz.
error: he disguises himself as a man named Gilles de Rais
correction: He does not disguises himself as Gilles de Rais.  He disguises himself as Malphus.  Gille de Rais is a servant of Dracula.
title: Castlevania

author: CowAboutThat
phrase_text: In the original Rayman and its closely related Gameboy Color remake, Rayman can free the cute pink spherical Electoons from the cages monsters have imprisoned them in. Later Rayman games also feature cages, but Rayman instead might be freeing lums, teensies, or Ssssam the Snake. Electoons make their grand return in the reboot Rayman Origins, back in familiar cartoonish cages.
error: and its closely related Gameboy Color remake
correction: Um, actually, in the Gameboy Color remake, the Electoons are just called Toons.
notes: https://www.raymanpc.com/wiki/en/Cage?__cf_chl_f_tk=TF6jlW7jWxKDBTr1chS5.IMvpz31KbdbDJliV_Ou8rI-1783183975-1.0.1.1-fN8T9wD5Vi4eIosZb93TbiAnVIkdTq5iTLUrqER.yrs
title: Rayman

author: CowAboutThat
phrase_text: Isaac Clarke begins Dead Space as a silent protagonist, but in Dead Space 2 and the games beyond, voice actor Gunner Wright lends his voice to the character, bringing to life Isaac’s waning grip on reality. The Marker, a giant artifact and the cause of necromorph outbreaks, also twists the minds of anyone nearby. Isaac is no exception, suffering from dementia and seeing hallucinations of his girlfriend Nicole. The choice to give Isaac a voice was generally well-received, so Wright was brought back to voice new lines for Isaac when the original was remade.
error: Isaac Clarke begins Dead Space as a silent protagonist
correction: Um, actually, while he doesn’t have lines in the original, he isn’t silent, as he’ll still make grunting noises and cries of pain.
title: Dead Space

author: JustZip
phrase_text: While episode tiebreaks are common enough, series tiebreaks are a lot rarer, only happening once as of 2026, in Series 20. At the end, Sanjeev Bhaskar came in last with 143 points, Phil Ellis came in fourth with 148 points, and Reece Shearsmith, Ania Magliano and Maisie Adam all tied with 151 points. After guessing how many T’s were hidden in a painting, Maisie Adam ended up becoming the champion of the Series.
error: Phil Ellis came in fourth with 148 points, and Reece Shearsmith, Ania Magliano and Maisie Adam all tied with 151 points.
correction: Reece Shearsmith actually came in fourth with 148 points, and Phil Ellis was part of the tiebreaker.
title: Taskmaster

author: Ymbirtt
phrase_text: In Mass Effect 1 and 2, melee combat was fairly vesitigal. Mass Effect 3 changed this, though. The Omni-Tool, a small portable computer with a holographic display, was repurposed into an Omni-Blade, which used hard-light technology to project a lethal knife from the user's wrist. Different character classes even used their Omni-Blades in different ways - the Engineer's is infused with fire, the Infiltrator's with arcing electricity, and the Vanguard forgoes the blade entirely for a giant biotically charged punch.
error: hard-light technology
correction: Um, actually, the Omni-Blade doesn't use hard-light technology. Omni-tools contain tiny manufacturing plants, effectively tiny 3D-printers, and an omni-blade is instantaneously manufactured at the moment the user initiates the attack.
notes: If you think you've seen people tapping on holographic displays in Mass Effect, that's not hard-light technology either. In fact, displays are holographically projected, the user's gloves - or in some enthusiasts' cases subdermal implants - simulate touch feedback as they push the projected buttons.
title: Mass Effect 3

author: Ymbirtt
phrase_text: Despite being released in 1999, Age of Empires II has continued to receive substantial re-releases and support right up to the present day. While it only released with 13 civilisations, the latest version allows players to play a diverse range of civilisations, active during the European middle ages, including the Mayans, Britons, Ethiopians, Ottomans, Indians and Vietnamese (with all the expansions, of course).
error: Indians
correction: Um, actually, the Indians are not a playable civilisation in the latest version. While they were playable in the past, they were later split into four civilisations because India did not exist as a unified nation during the European middle ages
title: Age of Empires II

author: Ymbirtt
phrase_text: While Age of Empires II takes a lot of historical liberties, one of its nicer flourishes is making sure that units of each civilisation speak historically appropriate languages. In keeping with its middle-aged setting, Frankish villagers greet you in middle French (Oé, que fais? Verax!), Briton monks recite Bible passages in middle English (Ye ben sauyd bi feith), and Celtic warriors call out middle Celtic battle cries (Cogadh, cogadh!)
error: Briton monks recite Bible passages in middle English
correction: Um, actually, while most Briton units speak middle English, Briton monks speak Latin. During the middle ages, England was predominantly Catholic, so monks would have studied the bible in Latin
title: Age of Empires II

author: Fluitaire
phrase_text: In the Novels of Temeraire, the Navy Captain William Laurence captures a dragon egg from a French frigate in the Napoleonic war. Upon hatching, the dragon and captain come to a fast friendship and go to Aerial Corp training. They then participate in the war, while also traveling to 5 of 7 continents, and weather many trials, including assassination attempts, turning traitor to their own country, and women.
error: Upon hatching, the dragon and captain come to a fast friendship and go to Aerial Corp training.
correction: The dragon imprints on the captain like a bird, and the Captain is dreading his new reality.
notes: The dragons in this series imprint on their Captains, but for the captain the Aerial Corp is considered a very disreputable branch of the military. The captain is not happy about having to take on the dragon and the relationship is a bit rocky at first. Though they do overcome this eventually.
title: The Novels of Temeraire

author: Ymbirtt
phrase_text: In Team Fortress 2's zany lore, real historical figures are replaced with far more Mann-ly equivalents. Picasso is now Kickasso, a painter famous for paintings produced during his "hunted in the jungle" period. Abraham Lincoln is now a mighty 6 foot 4 inch wrestler, famous for chokeslamming bullies. Shakespearicles is the strongest writer who ever lived, and is famous for inventing the rocket launcher and America
error: Abraham Lincoln is now a mighty 6 foot 4 inch wrestler, famous for chokeslamming bullies
correction: Um, actually, that's not part of TF2's zany lore. The actual real Abraham Lincoln was 6 foot 4 inches, and was famous for his wrestling skill. While he might not have invented the chokeslam, there are credible historical accounts of him picking up a local bully by his neck and shaking him about.
title: Team Fortress 2

author: CowAboutThat
phrase_text: In the episode Promortyus, Rick and Morty stumble across alien eggs that hatch and “facehugger” them, piloting their bodies with the personalities of the newborn aliens, though they maintain the voices of their hosts. Normally, these aliens have short life spans, quickly gestating and laying another egg, killing themselves and their hosts in the process. However, when they learn that Summer has a rare immunity to being taken over by them, they appoint her leader, and she decrees that they should live longer and build up a society instead, all as a ruse to get them to build her a spaceship to take Summer and her family off-planet.
error: Summer has a rare immunity to being taken over by them
correction: Um, actually, Summer isn’t immune, she just has a toothpick in her mouth that makes it impossible for the aliens to latch onto her face.
title: Rick and Morty

author: Fluitaire
phrase_text: In the Song of the Lioness quartet, the twins Alanna and Thom must leave home to become a lady and a knight respectively. Thom wishes to become a wizard, and Alanna wants to be a knight. They magic their servants and switch places with each other to pursue their dreams. Alanna becomes a knight by disguising her gender, and Thom becomes a powerful wizard by concealing his aptitude while at school. Both go on to become instrumental in saving the kingdom from the evil Duke Roger, the King's nephew.
error: They magic their servants and switch places with each other to pursue their dreams.
correction: They only need to threaten one of the servants with magic to convince him, the other casts a seeing spell to get a vision of the future which convinces her.
notes: Maude and Coram are the two servants. Maude is their secret magic teacher and is very amenable to their goals. Coram is against the idea but hates the idea of magic being used on him. Alanna only needs to threaten him with making him see things that aren't there with magic to get him on board.
title: The Song of the Lioness
